title: China's Largest Offshore Solar-Hydrogen Farm Starts Operation
content: China's largest integrated solar-hydrogen farm, the Rudong offshore photovoltaic-hydrogen energy storage project, has begun operations in Jiangsu Province, developed by Guohua Energy under CHN Energy. The 400 MW facility spans 287 hectares and features a 220 kV booster station, a 60 MW/120 MWh energy storage system, and a hydrogen production and refueling station. Once fully operational in 2025, it will generate 468 million kWh annually, saving 151,000 tonnes of coal and reducing 309,400 tonnes of COâ‚‚ emissions. The project integrates solar power, hydrogen production, and energy storage to enhance grid stability and peak-shaving capacity, marking a major step in Chinaâ€™s clean energy transition.
author: Analysts Account
